summary: Matched the SysDolphin baselib HSD performance-stat module by replacing asm/sysdolphin/baselib/perf.s with src/sysdolphin/baselib/perf.c and a new public header. The new C code defines HSD_PerfStat, HSD_PerfLastStat, HSD_PerfCurrentStat, start_time tracking, frame/stat reset logic, OSGetTime-based CPU/draw/total timing, and envelope-blending counting with an assert. obj_files.mk was updated to build the C object instead of the asm object, and a remaining asm reference in baselib_unknown_002.s was renamed from lbl_80407188 to HSD_PerfLastStat.
what_changed: Deleted the 277-line hand-written assembly file asm/sysdolphin/baselib/perf.s.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/perf.c implementing HSD_PerfInitStat, HSD_PerfSetStartTime, HSD_PerfSetCPUTime, HSD_PerfSetDrawTime, HSD_PerfSetTotalTime, and HSD_PerfCountEnvelopeBlending.
Added include/sysdolphin/baselib/perf.h declaring HSD_PerfStat and the HSD perf API.
Defined HSD_PerfStat with f32 cpu_time, draw_time, total_time, u32 unused, and u32 count[32], matching the 0x90-byte stat blocks present in the removed assembly.
Defined global HSD_PerfLastStat and HSD_PerfCurrentStat as zero-initialized HSD_PerfStat instances.
Replaced anonymous data label lbl_80407188 with the named symbol HSD_PerfLastStat in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002.s.
Changed obj_files.mk so the build uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/perf.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/perf.s.o.
smart_moves: The struct layout cleanly explains the assembly data: three timing floats plus an unused u32 before a 32-entry counter array, totaling 0x90 bytes.
HSD_PerfInitStat expresses the assembly copy-and-clear behavior directly as HSD_PerfLastStat = HSD_PerfCurrentStat followed by memset of HSD_PerfCurrentStat.

The repeated timing functions were reduced to readable C using OSGetTime() - start_time divided by a float conversion of (*(u32*)0x800000F8 / 4 / 60), matching the assembly’s conversion and frequency-scaling pattern.
The assertion string and line evidence from the asm were preserved as assert_line(0xA4, n < 32), keeping source-line-sensitive matching information.
Renaming lbl_80407188 to HSD_PerfLastStat in a neighboring asm file avoided leaving cross-file references to an obsolete anonymous label after the data moved to C.
decomp_lessons: When an asm file contains both functions and data blocks, matching the C source may require naming data symbols first so other asm files can continue to reference them.
Copy loops over fixed-size data blocks can often be represented by struct assignment if the inferred struct size and field layout match the original memory footprint.
Zeroing a whole stat object with memset can match a fixed-size assembly memset when sizeof(the inferred struct) equals the original byte count.

For performance/timing code, preserve exact hardware-register expressions and casts; the expression using address 0x800000F8, division by 4, division by 60, and an f32 cast is likely important for matching.
Source-line-sensitive assertions should use assert_line with the original line number rather than a generic assert when matching HSD/baselib code.
naming_conventions: HSD perf public functions use the HSD_Perf prefix, for example HSD_PerfInitStat and HSD_PerfSetCPUTime.
Global stat objects are named HSD_PerfLastStat and HSD_PerfCurrentStat.
The stat type is named HSD_PerfStat and is typedefed from struct _HSD_PerfStat.
The new header uses the include path sysdolphin/baselib/perf.h and a lowercase guard _hsd_perf_h_.
The counter field is named count and the timing fields use descriptive snake_case names: cpu_time, draw_time, total_time.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced perf.s with C only after its functions, globals, and data layout were represented in perf.c/perf.h.
Modeled the old lbl_80407188 data block as HSD_PerfLastStat and HSD_PerfCurrentStat as typed HSD_PerfStat globals.
Used struct assignment for the assembly’s 0x90-byte copy from current stat to last stat.
Used memset(&HSD_PerfCurrentStat, 0, sizeof(HSD_PerfStat)) to match the assembly memset of 0x90 bytes.
Kept the direct hardware memory read (*(u32*)0x800000F8) in the timing denominator to reproduce the assembly’s timer-frequency calculation.
Used assert_line(0xA4, n < 32) to preserve the assembly’s assert call with line 0xA4 and condition text n < 32.
Updated obj_files.mk from the asm object path to the C object path as the final build integration step.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review recorded, but the slice contains no review body or actionable reviewer feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that include sysdolphin/baselib/perf.h or use HSD_PerfStat fields.
Search for remaining lbl_80407188 references after PR 555 to confirm all references were renamed to HSD_PerfLastStat.
Compare other HSD timing modules that use (*(u32*)0x800000F8 / 4 / 60) for reusable matching patterns.
Search for assert_line usage with original HSD source line numbers in baselib decompilation work.
